questions.q: What has keys but no locks, space but no room, and you can enter but can't go inside?
What is full of keys but cannot open any door?
What comes once in a minute, twice in a moment, and never in one thousand years?
What can you see that is invisible?
What gets smaller every time you use it?
What can run but never walks, has a mouth but never talks, has a head but never weeps, has a bed but never sleeps?
What is seen in the middle of March and April that cannot be seen at any other time?
I am taken from a mine and shut up in a wooden case, from which I am never released, yet I am used by almost every person. What am I?
What word reads the same forwards and backwards and is the name of a female character in a famous TV show?
A man pushes his car to a hotel and tells the owner he is bankrupt. What happened?
What question can you never answer with the word 'yes'?
I speak without a mouth and hear without ears. I have no body, but I come alive with wind. What am I?
A woman has two sons who were born at the same hour on the same day of the same year, but they are not twins. How is this possible?
choices: They were born in different time zones
She had them in different hospitals

questions.q: What gets smaller every time it takes a bath?
A man walks into a bar and asks the bartender for a glass of water. The bartender pulls out a gun and points it at him. The man says thank you and leaves. What happened?
choices: The man had hiccups and the gun scared them away
The bartender was robbing the bar
The man was testing the bartender

questions.q: What can you catch but cannot throw?
A girl has as many brothers as she has sisters, but each brother has only half as many brothers as sisters. How many brothers and sisters are there?
What is always hot but cannot be eaten?
What do you call a female who is always aware of her husband's exact location?
Can you name three consecutive days without saying the words Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, or Sunday?
choices: This week, next week, the week after

questions.q: If all the walls, carpet, and furniture in a one-story house are pink, what color are the stairs?
What two words, when combined together, contain the most letters?
If you spell the word for bathing in water as S-O-A-K, how do you spell the white part of an egg?
What can you hold in your hand but can never be physically grasped or touched?
A man in all black walks down a dark country road at night. A car with its headlights off speeds around a corner and stops just before hitting him. How did the driver see him?
choices: The man was carrying a flashlight
The driver had night vision goggles

questions.q: What object has a thumb and four fingers but is not a living creature?

In a certain family, the father reads the newspaper, the mother cooks breakfast, the teenage son plays video games, and Uncle George retrieves the mail. One of these people just committed a crime on Sunday morning. Who is most likely guilty?
How many months have exactly 28 days?
If Noah built an ark and filled it with animals, how many animals did Moses take aboard?
What two words when spoken aloud sound like letters but are completely different?
What can be broken but never held in your hands?

A short man cannot reach the button for the 100th floor in his apartment building's elevator. On rainy days, he rides all the way up. On sunny days, he rides to the 50th floor and takes the stairs. Why?
choices: He likes exercise on sunny days
He is afraid of heights on rainy days
On rainy days he uses his umbrella handle to reach the button
The elevator is slower on sunny days

questions.q: What two keys can open absolutely no doors?
choices: A house key and a car key
A musical key and a digital key
A skeleton key and a spare key

questions.q: In a one-story pink house, if you place a cup on a table facing south while standing on the north side, where is the cup's handle?
choices: Always on the outside of the cup

questions.q: What has a hole in it but can still hold water?
You are driving a bus with many passengers. Along the route, people get on and off. What is the name of the bus driver?
choices: It depends on who the passengers are

questions.q: What cannot be eaten for breakfast, lunch, or dinner?
What has a crown but is not royalty?
If there are three apples and you take two away, how many do you have?
A doctor and a boy were fishing. The boy was the doctor's son, but the doctor was not the boy's father. What was the doctor's relationship to the boy?
If you throw a white stone into the Red Sea, what does it become?
What can travel around the whole world while staying in a corner?
If a plane crashes on the border between Canada and the United States, where are the survivors buried?
choices: Nowhere, because survivors are not buried

questions.q: A woman shoots her husband, then holds him underwater for five minutes. Right after, they enjoy a lovely dinner together. How is this possible?
choices: He was not actually her husband
She is a photographer and he is a swimmer
The woman is a photographer and shot a picture of him
He survived because he held his breath

questions.q: In a standard deck of playing cards, what is the only suit that does not contain a queen?
What is the only mammal that cannot jump?
In a criminal investigation, a detective finds a dead man in a closed room with a puddle of water and broken glass beside him. No signs of struggle are present. How did he die?
choices: He was poisoned by the water
He was a fish in a fishbowl that fell and broke
He attempted suicide by drowning in shallow water
He was killed by someone who escaped through a hidden passage

questions.q: A man enters a barbershop and asks the barber how much a haircut costs. The barber replies, 'Five dollars.' The man asks, 'How much for a shave?' The barber says, 'Three dollars.' The man then asks, 'How much to get my head painted?' The barber replies, 'Ten dollars.' Based on this information, what color is the barber's hair?
What has cities but no houses, forests but no trees, and water but no fish?

In the classic logic puzzle, five pirates divide 100 gold coins. The pirate ranking system is strict: the highest-ranked pirate proposes a division, and all pirates vote. If the proposal fails to achieve a majority, that pirate is killed and the next highest proposes. Assuming all pirates are perfectly logical and want to maximize their coins while staying alive, how many coins does the top-ranked pirate keep for himself?
A grandfather, father, and son go to a movie theater and pay for three tickets. How many people went to the movies?
If you have a bowl with five apples and you take three away, how many do you have in your hand?
In a race, you overtake the person in second place. What place are you in now?
choices: You cannot determine this without more information

questions.q: Two fathers and two sons go hunting and each catches one duck. They bring home three ducks total. How is this possible?
choices: One duck escaped and swam away
One person is both a father and a son (grandfather, father, and grandson)
They were hunting at two different locations
One of them caught two ducks

questions.q: A woman shoots a man and then dunks his head underwater. Moments later, they both walk away smiling. What happened?
choices: She shot him with a water gun while playing a game
She took his photograph and they went swimming
She is a magician and he was part of her act
She shot a photograph of him and they were celebrating

questions.q: In what year did Christmas fall on a Friday during the 20th century?
What five-letter word becomes shorter when you add two letters to it?
What word in the English language is spelled with three consecutive double letters?
A man enters a museum and looks at a painting. He says, 'That's my son.' The curator replies, 'But you have no son.' How is the painting of his son?
choices: The man is lying about being the father
The painting is of his adopted son
The man is the mother of the child

questions.q: What has a ring but no finger?
A man is found dead in the desert with a match in his hand. How did he die?
choices: He was struck by lightning from the match
The match was part of a murder weapon
He and other men drew straws and lost

questions.q: A woman has three adult daughters. The product of their ages is 36. The sum of their ages is the same as the house number. What are their ages?
A man is locked in a room with only a piano. How does he get out?
choices: He plays the piano to attract help
He uses the piano keys to unlock the door
He breaks the piano into pieces for tools
He plays a key on the piano

questions.q: A man dies and goes to heaven. Saint Peter shows him three doors. Behind door one is endless wealth, door two is endless pleasure, and door three is endless work. Which does he choose?
choices: Door three because he knows heaven wouldn't offer endless idleness
All doors lead to the same place

questions.q: What is always wet and can never dry out, no matter how long it sits in the sun?
If you spell out the numbers one through twelve, which is the only number whose letters appear in alphabetical order?
A man enters a room and dies. Later, investigators find him lying next to broken glass and water. There were no signs of struggle or poisoning. What is the most logical explanation?
choices: He was a fish who fell out of his bowl
He slipped on the water and hit his head
He committed suicide by drowning in the glass

questions.q: If you rearrange the letters of the word MOON, you can spell another celestial term. What is it?
What word in the English language becomes shorter when you add two letters to it?
A three-digit number has the property that when you reverse its digits, you get a number that is smaller than the original by 297. If the middle digit is 5, what is the original number?
What letter appears twice in the word SCISSORS?
A man is found hanging in an empty room with no furniture except a puddle of water beneath him. No one else was present. How did he die?
choices: He stood on a block of ice that melted
He jumped from a high window
He was poisoned by the water vapor
He was a stunt performer practicing a trick

questions.q: If you write down all numbers from 1 to 100, how many times does the digit 9 appear?
What is the only number that, when spelled out, has its letters in alphabetical order?
If a rooster is on a roof facing north and lays an egg, which direction does it roll?
What 6-letter word can have a letter taken from the middle to make a new word that is the opposite of the original?
If you have a bowl with six apples and you take away three, how many do you have?

A man is walking through the forest. He comes to a fork in the road. One path leads to certain death, the other to safety. Two guards stand at the fork—one always tells the truth, one always lies. The man does not know which guard is which. What single question can he ask one guard to determine the safe path?
choices: If I asked the other guard which path is safe, what would he say?
Do you always tell the truth?
Which path would the other guard recommend?

questions.q: What word becomes its opposite when you add the prefix 'un' to it, yet remains a valid English word in both forms?
What is the only number that, in English, has the same number of letters as its numerical value?
What seven-letter word is spelled identically forwards and backwards when you remove the first and last letters?
What is the name of the mythical creature that is represented by Scotland's national animal but does not exist in nature?
A person enters a room and no one is inside, yet there are three other people in the room. What is the logical explanation?
choices: The room is crowded and the person misspoke
Two of the other people are invisible
The person is looking in a mirror

questions.q: What is the only Shakespearean play title that contains no vowels other than 'o'?
If you write out all numbers from 1 to 1,000 in English words, how many times does the letter 'a' appear?
A man walks into a library and asks the librarian for books about paranoia. The librarian whispers, 'They're right behind you.' Why might the librarian say this?
choices: To scare the man as a prank
The paranoia section is physically located behind where the man is standing
The man is being followed by someone

questions.q: If all roses are flowers and all flowers fade, can you conclude that all roses fade?
choices: No, because some roses are artificial
Only if we're talking about cut roses

questions.q: A chemist was found murdered in his lab on a Monday morning. The police questioned three suspects: the janitor who arrived at 6 AM, the assistant who arrived at 8 AM, and the colleague who arrived at 10 AM. The janitor said he saw no one, the assistant said the chemist was alive when they left, and the colleague said the chemist was already dead. Who is the murderer?
choices: None of them - it was suicide

questions.q: What starts with 'T', ends with 'T', and has 'T' in it?
If you have a clock with three hands that are all identical and unmarked, how can you determine which hand is the hour hand?
choices: By watching the hand that moves the slowest
You cannot determine this with certainty
By the hand that points upward

questions.q: A person enters a room and the lights are off. Inside the room are three light switches, only one of which works. The switches control one light outside the room. You can flip the switches as many times as you want, but can only enter the room once. How do you determine which switch controls the light?
choices: Turn on two switches and watch for a pattern
Turn on the first switch, wait, turn it off, turn on the second switch, then enter the room and feel the bulbs
Enter the room multiple times while flipping switches
Use trial and error by entering several times

questions.q: Two people are born at the same moment in the same hospital, but one is older than the other. How?
choices: They are in different time zones
One was premature and one was full-term
They were born in different countries
Twins born at different minutes of the same hour

questions.q: If you were standing in Australia during December, what season would it be in Canada?
In what year did Iceland become the first country to legally recognize same-sex marriage?
Which famous physicist was born in Germany but became a Swiss and American citizen during his lifetime?
In what decade did the first transatlantic telegraph cable successfully transmit a message?
If you rearrange all the letters in the word 'dormitory,' what two related words can you make?
Which Nobel Prize category was added to the original five awards in 1969?
In which country was the sport of curling first documented as being played on ice?
If you spell out the word 'FOUR,' how many letters have straight lines only?
What country's flag is the only one that has more sides than a rectangle?
In what year did the Oxford English Dictionary first include the word 'selfie'?
What mathematical symbol did Italian mathematician Leonardo Fibonacci use but never actually published?
Which desert is the only one to span three countries: Egypt, Sudan, and Libya?
A woman shoots her husband, then holds him underwater for five minutes. Right after, they go out to dinner together. How?
choices: She shot a photo and he was playing dead
She used a water gun and he held his breath
She shot a dart gun and he survived
It was a staged magic trick

questions.q: What English word becomes shorter when you add two letters to it?
A man is found dead in a sealed room with only a puddle of water and some scattered pieces of wood nearby. What happened?
choices: He stood on a block of ice that melted as he hanged himself
He was hit by pieces of wood

questions.q: If you spell out every number from 0 to 1,000, how many times does the letter 'A' appear?
A man walks into a bar and orders a drink. He takes one sip and leaves money on the counter, but never drinks the rest. Why?
choices: He recognized someone he wanted to avoid
There was a fly in the drink
He saw his reflection and realized it wasn't a bar

questions.q: A man is in a sealed room with nothing but a table and a mirror. How does he escape?
choices: He looks in the mirror, sees what he saw, takes the saw and cuts the table in half
He breaks the mirror and uses the shards
He climbs on the table and reaches a window
There is no escape from a sealed room

questions.q: A deaf person and a blind person enter a shop together. Only one can make a purchase. Who and why?
choices: The deaf person, because they can see the products
The blind person, because they can hear prices
Both can make purchases; disability doesn't prevent shopping
Neither can make purchases without assistance

questions.q: What five-letter word becomes a different word when you remove the first letter, and yet another different word when you remove the last letter?
What three-letter word refers to a young person but becomes an adult's activity when you rearrange its letters?
You can see it, but you cannot touch it. If you try to catch it, it disappears. What is it?
What is always coming but never arrives?
What gets wetter the more it dries?
What word is spelled incorrectly in every single dictionary?

source.notes: The word 'incorrectly' is literally spelled 'i-n-c-o-r-r-e-c-t-l-y' — even in the dictionary.

questions.q: What lives if it's fed, but dies if you give it a drink?
What goes up but never comes down?
If you have it, you want to share it. Once you share it, you don't have it. What is it?
What starts with 'e' and ends with 'e' but contains only one letter?

source.notes: An envelope holds a single letter inside.

questions.q: How can a man go 25 days without sleeping?
choices: He sleeps at night, not during the day

questions.q: If you only had one match in a dark room with an oil lamp, kindling, and newspaper, what would you light first?
If you have a basket with 6 apples and take 4 away, how many do you have?
What's greater than God, more evil than the devil, the rich need it, the poor have it — and if you eat it, you'll die?

source.notes: Nothing is greater than God; nothing is more evil than the devil; the rich need nothing; the poor have nothing; if you eat nothing, you'll die.

questions.q: Is it legal for a man to marry his widow's sister?
choices: No, but you'd need a permit
No — if a man has a widow he is dead

source.original_a: No, but since he is dead it would be hard to do so

questions.q: A cowboy rode into town on Friday, stayed three days, and rode out on Friday. How?
